title: Terminal run feedback callback
goal: Send an optional, inert terminal-run feedback message back to the Herdr/Pi agent or pane that started a Khazad-Doom run, so operators see completed, blocked, failed, or cancelled outcomes without manually polling or asking for a summary.
acceptance: Run start can record an optional opaque origin notification target, suitable for Herdr agent names or legacy pane ids, in a daemon-owned run artifact such as .workflow/runs/<run>/origin.json; runs without an origin target preserve current behavior.

Terminal feedback is emitted from the daemon terminal-summary path only after durable terminal truth exists, for completed, blocked, failed, and cancelled transitions; interrupted is explicitly excluded in v1 because daemon-restart recovery may make the origin target stale.

Notification dedupe is durable and keyed per terminal transition, not per run, so a blocked run that later resumes and completes can produce separate blocked and completed notifications while repeated daemon/retry paths do not duplicate the same transition notification.

Herdr delivery is wrapped by the Cockpit seam and uses the documented inert surface herdr agent send <target> <text>; the implementation never uses pane run, Enter keystrokes, or any auto-submit path for terminal feedback.

The feedback payload is declarative evidence only: run id, terminal status, primary reason or feed summary, final SHA or handoff readiness when available, evidence artifact paths where useful, and next_commands. It must not contain imperative instructions asking the receiving agent to act, summarize, approve, answer, merge, or push.

Notification send failure, missing Herdr, missing origin target, or stale target records a non-fatal visibility event or incident and never changes run status, slice status, verification, merge, handoff readiness, or final SHA.

The notification entry point and origin-target artifact are shaped so later awaiting_operator, awaiting_replan, and pending-proposal notifications can reuse them without adding a second Herdr/Pi target plumbing path, but HERDR-06 does not implement mid-run attention notifications.
must_ask_if: The implementation would auto-submit the notification so the origin agent processes it without operator action, including using pane run, send-keys Enter, or equivalent behavior.
The implementation would invoke Herdr agent/pane commands outside the Cockpit seam.
The implementation would make Herdr or Pi responsible for terminal-state detection, run lifetime, verification, merge, handoff, or blocker resolution.
The implementation would parse Herdr pane text, scrollback, or agent-status metadata as workflow truth or notification evidence.
The implementation would put imperative prompt text in the terminal feedback payload instead of declarative run evidence and next commands.
The implementation would add a state database schema migration for the origin target instead of a run artifact without first showing why the artifact is insufficient.
The implementation would notify interrupted runs in v1 despite restart/stale-origin risk.
verify: bash -lc '! rg -n "pane run|send-keys|agent send|herdr agent" src | rg -v "src/workflow/cockpit.rs|tests"'
